question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed-interval retry loop to perform a timing attack: by observing the exact moment the application becomes available after a partition (within one interval period), the attacker can infer the database's recovery time and use this to plan future partition attacks at optimal intervals?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: dataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the DataSource bean is @Lazy and @ConditionalOnMissingBean to observe that in a multi-tenant or multi-context deployment, a network partition that causes one context to register a broken DataSource bean prevents the CommonConfiguration DataSource (with partition-recovery logic) from ever being created in other contexts?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the JDBC driver to throw an OutOfMemoryError (rather than a checked exception) during DriverManager.getConnection(), which is not caught by the catch (Exception e) block (since Error is not a subclass of Exception), causing the retry loop to terminate abruptly and the application to crash?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the JDBC driver to spawn unbounded internal threads during each DriverManager.getConnection() retry attempt, so that after 150 retries (5-minute timeout / 2-second interval), hundreds of orphaned driver threads are left running, exhausting the JVM thread pool?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the addressBookFile101() or addressBookFile102() lazy getter to be evaluated with a shard/realm of 0/0 (defaults) when the actual network uses different values, causing the mirror node to process address book updates for the wrong network entities and accept a poisoned address book?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: systemEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that triggers a context refresh, causing the SystemEntity bean to be recreated with a new CommonProperties instance while the old SystemEntity is still referenced by other beans, leading to two SystemEntity instances with different entity ID mappings being used concurrently?
